content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAi. PRACTICES COMMISSION 
1102 Q Street• Suite 3000 • Sacrnmcnto, CA 9581 t 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) :322-0886 
Daniel G. Sodergren 
City Attorney 
City of Pleasanton 
POBox520 
Pleasanton, CA 94566-0802 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. W-17-158 
Dear Mr. Sodergren: 
October 2, 2017 
This letter is written notification that we have withdrawn the above-captioned request for 
advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act. 
Under Regulation 18329.5, the Commission will not render advice regarding the 
interpretation of an agency's conflict-of-interest code or the application of that code to a specific 
individual unless certain requirements are met. As the "code reviewing body" the City Council of 
the City of Pleasanton makes the determination regarding whether or not members of the Economic 
Vitality Committee are "designated employees" subject to the city's conflict-of-interest code. 
Accordingly, we decline to issue advice in this matter and consider the request withdrawn. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
By: 
ZWN:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Jack C. Woodside 
General Counsel 
' 
^mry W. Norton 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

As the "code reviewing body" the City Council of the City of Pleasanton makes the determination regarding whether or not members of the Economic Vitality Committee are "designated employees" subject to the city's conflict-of-interest code. Accordingly, we decline to issue advice in this matter and consider the request withdrawn.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether members of the Economic Vitality Committee are designated employees subject to the City of Pleasanton's conflict-of-interest code.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to issue advice and withdraws the request because the City Council of Pleasanton, as the code reviewing body, has authority to make determinations about whether committee members are designated employees under the city's conflict-of-interest code.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Withdrawal letter with clear explanation of basis for declination. No separate QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections but substantive legal reasoning provided in body of letter. File number W-17-158 indicates this is a withdrawn request.

CONCLUSION: Under Regulation 18329.5, the Commission will not render advice regarding the interpretation of an agency's conflict-of-interest code or the application of that code to a specific individual unless certain requirements are met. As the "code reviewing body" the City Council of the City of Pleasanton makes the determination regarding whether or not members of the Economic Vitality Committee are "designated employees" subject to the city's conflict-of-interest code. Accordingly, we decline to issue advice in this matter and consider the request withdrawn.

embedding.summary: The FPPC withdrew a request for advice from the City Attorney of Pleasanton regarding whether members of the Economic Vitality Committee are designated employees subject to the city's conflict-of-interest code, citing Regulation 18329.5 limitations and the City Council's authority as the code reviewing body.
